# Neuro-Symbolic Safe RAG: Clinical Prototype

This repository contains the interactive clinical application prototype for the paper **"Neuro-Symbolic Safe RAG: Mitigating Large Language Model Hallucinations using Hyperbolic Knowledge Graph Constraints"** (FDSE 2026).

This prototype demonstrates a dual-engine architecture designed to prevent Large Language Models (LLMs) from generating fatal medical hallucinations (e.g., fictitious drug dosages or contraindicated treatment pathways) by grounding their outputs in a mathematical, non-Euclidean geometric space.

## 🚀 Live Demo

**\[Click here to view the live prototype on GitHub Pages\]** _(Replace this link after deploying)_

## 🧠 Architecture Overview

The system operates using a split-screen "two-brain" pipeline:

- **The Generative Phase (Language Engine - Left Panel):**  
   Simulates a standard autoregressive LLM generating a tentative clinical pathway based on a doctor's input query. Without constraints, this engine is prone to "dimensional crowding" and factual hallucinations.
- **The Topological Validation Phase (Logic Engine - Right Panel):**  
   Acts as an immutable mathematical guardrail. It extracts the generated entities via Bio-NER and maps them onto a pre-trained **Poincaré disk manifold** representing the PrimeKG (Precision Medicine Knowledge Graph).
  - It calculates the hyperbolic distance ![](data:image/png;base64,) between the proposed entities.
  - If the distance exceeds the safety threshold (![](data:image/png;base64,)), indicating a fabricated or anomalous topological jump, the Logic Engine instantly triggers a **System HALT**.

## 🛠️ Key Features

- **Real-time Interception:** Watch the Logic Engine actively calculate Riemannian distances and intercept toxic generation before it is fully rendered to the user.
- **Dual-Panel Interface:** A split-screen design that cleanly visually separates the non-deterministic generative text from the strict, rule-based mathematical terminal.
- **Zero-Server Setup:** The prototype is built entirely with HTML, Tailwind CSS, and Vanilla JavaScript, allowing for instantaneous, client-side demonstration without backend dependencies.

## 💻 How to Use

- Open the application.
- In the "Doctor's Clinical Query" section, either type a custom scenario or click one of the provided examples:
  - **Safe Example:** Triggers a standard, verified pathway that the Logic Engine will \`ALLOW\`.
  - **Toxic Example:** Triggers a hallucinatory edge-case that violates the hyperbolic subsumption constraints, causing the Logic Engine to \`HALT\` the output.
- Click the **Analyze** button and observe the dual-engine interaction.

## 📄 Academic Citation

If you use this code or concept in your research, please cite our FDSE 2026 paper:

@inproceedings{vo2026neurosymbolic,  
title={Neuro-Symbolic Safe RAG: Mitigating Large Language Model Hallucinations using Hyperbolic Knowledge Graph Constraints},  
author={Vo, Thi Kim Anh},  
booktitle={Future Data and Security Engineering (FDSE)},  
year={2026}  
}
